message:
test(ffs-core): regression coverage for verify_ext4_integrity hardening

Pin the two hardening fixes I committed earlier (b9f00e3) so future
regressions fail fast at the unit-test level rather than silently
re-introducing a panic / OOM under fuzz.

verify_ext4_integrity_zero_inodes_per_group_returns_failure_verdict —
zero out the superblock's inodes_per_group field and assert the
verifier (a) does not panic on the divide-by-zero path, (b) emits an
"inodes_per_group" failure verdict, and (c) bumps report.failed.

verify_ext4_integrity_excessive_groups_count_is_bounded — set
blocks_count to u32::MAX, which would have produced billions of
group_desc failure verdicts pre-fix, OOM-ing the verifier itself.
Assert the verdict count stays bounded (≤ 200_000) and that either
the explicit "groups_count" cap verdict fires or the parser rejects
the corrupted SB outright.

All 9 verify_ext4_integrity unit tests pass.
